meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding to Solve Project Stalls
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on freelance projects. Learn to implement high-converting user onboarding using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver faster and impress clients.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is often a lack of user momentum. By implementing a structured onboarding flow, you guide users to their 'Aha!' moment immediately. Using Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can automate this process, ensuring your projects ship faster and retain clients.
content.intro:
The graveyard of freelance careers is littered with half-finished prototypes and stalled MVPs. You know the feeling: the initial spark of the project fades into the grind of endless edge-case handling, and suddenly, the deadline feels like a closing trap. You are stuck because you are building features, not user success journeys.

True velocity doesn't come from writing more code; it comes from building systems that guide users to value. By shifting your focus to high-impact user onboarding, you transform a complex, intimidating application into a clear, guided path. This isn't just about design—it's about engineering a workflow that keeps your clients happy and your projects moving toward the finish line.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Before writing code, map the exact action that provides immediate value to your user. This prevents feature creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project scope and identify the top three user actions that constitute the 'Aha!' moment. Create a minimal onboarding flow that leads the user directly to these actions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'map user journey to core value proposition'

steps.title: Scaffold the Onboarding UI
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, responsive onboarding modal that doesn't block the core UI but guides the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular, reusable onboarding component using Tailwind CSS that guides users through a 3-step setup process.

steps.title: Implement State-Driven Progress
steps.description: Track onboarding completion to ensure users don't get stuck re-seeing the same hints. Use local storage to persist state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a hook 'useOnboarding' that manages the state of the onboarding steps and persists the progress to localStorage.

steps.title: Integrate Contextual Tooltips
steps.description: Use Claude Code to inject tooltips at critical junctions where users typically drop off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a configuration file for contextual tooltips that trigger based on user behavior in the dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'inject contextual tooltips at high-churn points'

steps.title: Automate Onboarding Analytics
steps.description: Track completion rates to validate if your onboarding is actually solving the 'can't finish' problem for the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a telemetry wrapper to the onboarding flow that sends events to a simple analytics endpoint whenever a step is completed.

pro_tips: Never force an account creation before showing value; use 'guest mode' to build momentum first.
Keep onboarding under 30 seconds; if it takes longer, you are teaching, not helping.
Use progressive disclosure to show only the settings required for the current task.
Always provide a 'skip' button; users hate feeling trapped in a tutorial.
Measure the time-to-first-value (TTFV) as your primary metric for project success.
content.next_steps: Review your onboarding analytics after 48 hours. If drop-off remains high, iterate on the 'Aha!' moment definition using the same Claude Code workflow.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your projects stall. By automating the user journey, you save time, reduce technical debt, and deliver a polished, professional product that keeps clients coming back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
